project_identity.name: BuildContextMemory
project_identity.description: A lightweight system that preserves project memory and AI behavior consistency across sessions and tools.
project_identity.architecture_summary: Two-file system: AI_Starter.md defines AI behavior; Build-Context-Memory.json stores append-only project/session memory.

decisions_log.decision: Use deterministic hashing for privacy abstraction
decisions_log.reason: Keeps demo behavior stable while avoiding raw token storage in the memory layer.
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Store raw identifiers; use random UUIDs per request; defer privacy handling to later stage

decisions_log.decision: Use heuristic simulation for the first pass
decisions_log.reason: Keeps the endpoint runnable and demo-ready while preserving the architecture contract for future LLM-driven simulation.

decisions_log.decision: Isolate tests with a dedicated SQLite file
decisions_log.reason: Prevents state leakage across TestClient runs and makes the smoke tests deterministic.
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Use in-memory sqlite; clear the production db file between tests

decisions_log.decision: Lightweight orchestrator instead of full LangGraph (for now)
decisions_log.reason: Dependency-light, fast iteration; can migrate to full LangGraph later
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Full LangGraph integration with graph.add_node/add_edge; stateful execution

decisions_log.reason: Aligns with modern Python async/await ecosystem; httpx is lightweight and compatible
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: requests (sync only); aiohttp (more complex); gRPC (adds complexity)

sessions.last_session_summary: Implemented comprehensive testing infrastructure including performance benchmarks, CI/CD pipeline, integration tests, API docs generator, and demo recording script. All core API endpoints (ingest, simulate, recommend, explain) are functional and ready for performance tuning.

Comprehensive test orchestration with unit, integration, performance, and coverage reporting
End-to-end demo flow demonstrating ingest → simulate → recommend → explain with performance metrics

decisions_log.reason: Hackathon submission requires demonstrating system performance, latency SLAs, and throughput capabilities

decisions_log.reason: Enable continuous testing, coverage tracking, and automated security checks for production readiness
decisions_log.alternatives_considered: Manual CI/CD setup; GitLab CI; Jenkins
